# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent rabbitmq management exposed.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3.8'
services:
  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3-management
    container_name: rabbitmq
    environment:
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS}
      RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE: ${RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE}
      RABBITMQ_NODENAME: rabbit@rabbitmq
    ports:
      - "5672:5672"
    networks:
      - rabbitmq_network
    volumes:
      - rabbitmq_data:/var/lib/rabbitmq
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
      update_config:
        parallelism: 1
        delay: 10s
      restart_policy:
        condition: on-failure

  rabbitmq-management:
    image: rabbitmq:3-management
    container_name: rabbitmq_management
    environment:
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER}
      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS: ${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS}
    ports:
      - "15672:15672"
    networks:
      - rabbitmq_network
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
      update_config:
        parallelism: 1
        delay: 10s
      restart_policy:
        condition: on-failure
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.rabbitmq.rule=Host(`${RABBITMQ_MANAGEMENT_HOST}`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.rabbitmq.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.rabbitmq.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.rabbitmq.tls.certresolver=myresolver"

networks:
  rabbitmq_network:
    driver: overlay

volumes:
  rabbitmq_data:
